extra.reason: Set kubeflow-profiles/oauth2-proxy chart admin defaults to empty; externalized kiali root_domain to a variable wired from account module. grep clean, hclfmt+fmt clean.

Replaced misleading inbound-cidrs=0.0.0.0/0 comment (grafana, kiali) and added matching accurate comment (dex, dashboard) across all four shared-ALB admin ingresses. Comment-only; value unchanged. terraform fmt clean on all four modules.

Wrapped exec in dynamic block gated on var.kube_data_auth_enabled in common/kube-provider.hcl and common/helm-provider.hcl (apply/flag=true keeps exec token-expiry fix; plan/flag=false drops exec -> kubeconfig fallback). Moved route53 zone lookup into account module (data.aws_route53_zone.root + output root_zone_id); added mock root_zone_id; rewired dex/prometheus/kiali/kubeflow to consume var.root_zone_id from dependency.account.outputs.root_zone_id instead of their own live lookup. make plan-all now plans all modules cleanly: account does the single live lookup (Z0873790MAHSTW1NBQTC), consumers plan with mock zone_id (acm validation record known-after-apply). terraform fmt + terragrunt hclfmt clean.

All 3 workflows: added 'Install Terraform' step (hashicorp/setup-terraform@b9cd54a v3.1.2, terraform_version from env tf_version=1.9.8, wrapper off) after AWS creds; replaced tofu_version/tf_version action inputs with tf_path: terraform (satisfies action.yml mise-check and makes Terragrunt use the terraform binary; default tfpath is terraform anyway). env renamed tofu_version->tf_version. .tool-versions unchanged (still terraform 1.9.8). YAML validated. Not committed.

Migrated Terragrunt 0.86.2 CLI flags in Makefile (~18 lines) and 3 CI workflows: run-all <cmd> -> <cmd> --all; --terragrunt-non-interactive -> --non-interactive; --terragrunt-include-dir -> --queue-include-dir; --terragrunt-strict-include -> --queue-strict-include; hclfmt --terragrunt-check --terragrunt-diff -> hcl fmt --check --diff. FIXED & VALIDATED LIVE. Migrated profile-controller CompositeController->DecoratorController (+ sync.py object/attachments interface, + checksum annotation, pipelines chart 1.1.6). User deployed via terraform. Post-deploy: DecoratorController present, CompositeController pruned, webhook restarted with new sync.py. metacontroller: 0 reconciler errors (was 100% 'namespaces. not found'). Reconciliation confirmed: deleting ml-pipeline-ui-artifact auto-recreates it in ~4s at ghcr.io/kubeflow/kfp-frontend:2.16.1 (ready), now controller-owned. Both per-namespace pods (artifact+viz) managed at 2.16.1. Root cause was the v2.0.5->v4.11.22 metacontroller bump (during pipelines upgrade) without the matching upstream Composite->Decorator migration.

FIXED & VALIDATED on deployed cluster. Root cause: per-profile artifact proxy read mlpipeline-minio-artifact from namespace 'kubeflow' (default) instead of its own 'kubeflow-admin' because FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE was unset -> default-editor cross-ns 403 -> swallowed -> generic 'fetch provider secret' 500. Captured via patched 2nd frontend instance. Fix: added FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE: namespace to the artifact deployment in sync.py (matches upstream KFP), pipelines 1.1.7. After deploy + a DecoratorController re-sync, the artifact pod has FRONTEND_SERVER_NAMESPACE=kubeflow-admin and /artifacts/get returns HTTP 200 (seq + concurrent) with content. NOT ambient/version/CPU/waypoint (all red herrings). NOTE: existing profile namespaces pick up sync.py changes on the next DecoratorController resync (hourly) or a namespace event; I forced it via a namespace annotation.

RESOLVED (same root cause as platform-0vu). The metacontroller 'namespaces. not found' reconcile failure was fixed by migrating the profile-controller CompositeController->DecoratorController (metacontroller v4.x dropped core-resource CompositeController parents; KFP's own fix). Now confirmed the second half of acceptance: removed the manual stopgap kfp-launcher (label stopgap=platform-etk-manual) from kubeflow-admin and forced a DecoratorController re-sync -> the controller AUTO-PROVISIONED kfp-launcher in ~3s, now owned by the namespace (controller-managed, auto-heals), with correct content (defaultPipelineRoot=minio://mlpipeline/v2/artifacts, providers fromEnv:false + secretRef mlpipeline-minio-artifact, endpoint minio-service.kubeflow:9000). KFP_DEFAULT_PIPELINE_ROOT is set so the controller emits the kfp-launcher child. Per-namespace KFP provisioning is fully working.

COMPLETE & verified. Ambient dataplane labels are codified in IaC and live state matches (zero drift): (1) kubeflow ns -> terraform/main.tf:19 istio.io/dataplane-mode=ambient, no istio-injection (with explanatory comment); (2) profile namespaces -> kubeflow-profiles profiles-namespace-labels-data configmap sets dataplane-mode=ambient + use-waypoint=waypoint, no istio-injection; the profile-controller setNamespaceLabels is additive so it won't re-add istio-injection. Live: kubeflow + kubeflow-admin both dataplane=ambient, injection empty. ml-pipeline-ui restrictive policy (oauth2-proxy principal, not allow-all) is in the chart and the pipelines UI loads (verified extensively this session). Fresh installs get correct labels via the admin-namespace.yaml pre-install hook; no manual kubectl label needed (the one-time istio-injection- removal this session was legacy-state cleanup). NOTE: the bead's original 2026-06-22/23 'dataplane split' notes were superseded by the xd4 waypoint work — profile namespaces now run ambient+waypoint, which is what's codified.

DONE & verified (chart + live). ml-pipeline-ui AuthorizationPolicy is the restrictive oauth2-proxy-SA policy: chart authorization_policies.yaml -> action ALLOW, principal cluster.local/ns/{{ .Values.kubeflow.namespace }}/sa/oauth2-proxy, with corrected comment (oauth2-proxy reverse-proxies /pipeline/, not straight-from-gateway). Live deployed policy matches: principals=[cluster.local/ns/kubeflow/sa/oauth2-proxy], selector app=ml-pipeline-ui, no allow-all rule (no regression). Pipelines UI loads without 'RBAC: access denied' — verified end-to-end this session. Codification caveat handled by platform-iyr (closed); blocked platform-nkx already closed.

DONE & verified live. Replaced the '- {}' allow-all in minio-service and metadata-grpc-service with scoped source principals; service-cache-server intentionally keeps allow-all (kube-apiserver webhook, out of mesh). EVIDENCE: ran live pipelines with ztunnel access logging (Telemetry API) -> the step-pod callers to BOTH minio-service and metadata-grpc-service are ns/<profile>/sa/default-editor (confirmed: kubeflow-admin/default-editor, 3x minio + 6x metadata). Persistent callers use pooled conns (didn't emit connection-complete), so combined the evidence with the chart's documented caller comments + existing policy principals. SCOPED LISTS: minio-service = ml-pipeline, ml-pipeline-ui, ml-pipeline-persistenceagent, ml-pipeline-visualizationserver, kubeflow-pipelines-cache + per-profile default-editor (range over .Values.profile_namespaces). metadata-grpc-service = ml-pipeline, kubeflow-pipelines-metadata-writer, kubeflow-pipelines-cache, ns/kubeflow/sa/default (metadata-envoy runs as default SA, fronts the external /ml_metadata route) + per-profile default-editor. VERIFIED: applied the scoped policies live, ran a pipeline -> Succeeded (artifact writes + metadata logging OK), artifact preview HTTP 200 (minio read by default-editor proxy), zero authz denials. Pipelines chart 1.1.7->1.1.8 + tf pin. Live policies are kubectl-applied (preview); a terraform apply of 1.1.8 reconciles them. RESIDUAL (minor): metadata's ns/kubeflow/sa/default is the loosest principal (metadata-envoy uses the default SA) — a future tightening could give metadata-envoy a dedicated SA.

Fixed in chart 1.3.0: renamed SeaweedFS admin user kubeflow-admin -> seaweedfs-admin in the postStart so the admin identity no longer collides with the kubeflow-admin profile namespace (auth is by access key, name is internal-only). Validated end-to-end: two distinct identities (seaweedfs-admin=Admin matching central secret; kubeflow-admin=scoped with its own key 2RKR...); reconciles idempotent (no admin clobber/key churn); a real KFP v2 pipeline ran SUCCEEDED with artifacts written under private-artifacts/kubeflow-admin/ (scoped key + prefixed LIST confirmed, bucket top-level only private-artifacts = isolation). Validation pipeline kept at examples/pipelines/seaweedfs_artifact_check.py.

Hardened oauth2-proxy X-Forwarded-* trust (PR #196 merged+deployed). Set trusted_proxy_ips=[VPC CIDR, 127.0.0.0/8] via var.vpc_cidr_block; loopback added per review for sidecar-injection robustness. Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: running configmap shows trusted_proxy_ips=["10.0.0.0/16","127.0.0.0/8"]; pod rolled (config checksum) 1/1 Running 0 restarts; startup --trusted-proxy-ip WARNING gone; auth flow intact (unauth request -> 302 Dex); real-client-IP STILL honored (marked probe logged client IP 97.126.161.20 == my egress, not gateway/loopback -> XFF correctly trusted from gateway peer in VPC CIDR). Determined peer empirically via ztunnel access logs (src.addr=ingress-gateway pod IP under ambient). Follow-up platform-5wb filed to remove the stale inject annotation.

Removed stale sidecar.istio.io/inject annotation from oauth2-proxy deployment (PR #198 merged+deployed). Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: pod rolled (new pod oauth2-proxy-5b97f4ddd8-6vcdf), single container, 1/1 Running 0 restarts; running pod has no inject annotation (inject=[]); still ambient-captured + authz-enforced (new pod served an ingress-gateway-originated request gated by the ingress-gateway-principal AuthorizationPolicy, which is a ztunnel/ambient L4 control -> proves capture); login flow intact (302 -> Dex authorize) and real client IP still honored (logged 97.126.161.20). checksum/config annotation retained.

Already completed via the earlier 'adopt seaweedfs' work (commit series 430b981..e20ed26, merged to main) — not still applicable as an open task. Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: SeaweedFS is the sole artifact store (deployment seaweedfs 1/1, svc exposing 8333 S3/9000/8111 master, seaweedfs-pvc 20Gi Bound, ~5h uptime); profile-controller sync.py provisions per-namespace creds via the SeaweedFS IAM API; artifactRepository object_store_endpoint=seaweedfs:9000. MinIO fully removed: orphaned-resource sweep found NO minio Deployment/StatefulSet/Service/PVC/Pod in kubeflow or kubeflow-admin and NO minio PVs/EBS cluster-wide. Bead scope item 'retire vestigial TF random_password.minio_access_key/secret_key' is a non-issue: those were REPURPOSED as the SeaweedFS admin S3 creds (legacy naming retained by design). Remaining 'minio' strings are load-bearing legacy names (the mlpipeline-minio-artifact secret name is what KFP/Argo expect) — a cosmetic rename would be risky churn on the live cred path with no functional gain, so intentionally not done.

Added non-root securityContext to KFP profile-controller, matching upstream KFP 2.16.1 + a hardening improvement from PR #199 review (runAsGroup 0 -> 1000, since upstream's GID 0 is OpenShift convention not needed on EKS). PR #199 merged+deployed. Verified live on archegos-dev-eks: new pod 1/1 Running, 0 restarts (no crashloop); effective securityContext exactly as intended (pod: runAsNonRoot+RuntimeDefault seccomp; container: runAsUser 1000, runAsGroup 1000, allowPrivilegeEscalation false, capabilities drop ALL, seccomp RuntimeDefault). Proven non-root (runAsNonRoot:true + Running = kubelet enforced). sync.py reconciles correctly as UID/GID 1000 (metacontroller POST /sync -> HTTP 200, no permission errors/tracebacks). All acceptance criteria met.

CI helm lint/template/kubeconform workflow for kubeflow charts merged via PR #200. Workflow self-validated green on its own PR (Charts - Helm Validate: success) and proven to fail on invalid manifests (kubeconform exit 1). Includes Copilot-review hardening: --include-crds (renders the 20 CRDs in pipelines/training-operator/kubeflow-profiles), -kubernetes-version 1.33.0 (deterministic, cluster-aligned), pv-fsx ci-values fixture. Now active on future PRs touching kubeflow/charts/**.

Fixed via PR #201 (merged). common/mocks.hcl eks mock cluster_version bumped 1.31 -> 1.33 on main, now matching eks/cluster/terragrunt.hcl kubernetes_version=1.33. Mock-only change (affects terragrunt plan/validate mock_outputs when the real eks output is unavailable, e.g. the Infra Validate & Plan CI job feeding the CSI addon modules' kubernetes_version); no runtime resources, so no live-cluster deploy/verification applicable. Consistency confirmed on origin/main.

Superseded / no longer applicable. The hardening this bead targeted was achieved via the ambient-mesh migration + platform-f2i (CLOSED), NOT the sidecar-injection + STRICT mTLS architectural change this bead assumed was required. Premise change: ambient ztunnel capture gives inject=false KFP step pods + metadata-envoy an L4 SPIFFE identity (ns/<profile>/sa/default-editor, ztunnel-confirmed live), so the AuthorizationPolicies were scoped to source principals WITHOUT injecting any sidecars. Current state (chart + live-verified on archegos-dev-eks): metadata-grpc-service = SCOPED (ml-pipeline, metadata-writer, cache, dedicated sa/metadata-envoy [PR #185], per-profile default-editor); minio-service = REMOVED, replaced by seaweedfs policy which is SCOPED + split by port (8333 S3 data plane, 8111 IAM control plane); service-cache-server = intentionally/permanently allow-all (- {}) because its caller is kube-apiserver's cache MutatingWebhook, a control-plane component with no mesh identity in ANY dataplane mode -> the bead's proposed sidecar+mTLS fix could not scope it either (documented as a permanent justification in authorization_policies.yaml). All three targets addressed; nothing actionable remains.

Deployed + verified live on archegos-dev-eks (PR #209, chart 1.4.2, release rev 20). mysql now uses a terraform-generated MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD (mysql-secret password non-empty, b64 len 44 = 32 bytes), MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD removed, PVC cut v2->v3 forcing a fresh datadir so the password applies on first bootstrap. Followed up by restarting the DB-client deployments (ml-pipeline/cache-server/metadata-grpc) so they re-read the rotated secret; they reconnected with no auth errors (ml-pipeline: 'Client manager initialized successfully' + 'Starting RPC server'; cache-server Running 0 restarts). NOTE the v3 cutover wiped prior KFP run history (expected, fresh-deploy model). Stacked-PR gotcha: #209 merged into the revert branch not main; re-landed via a direct merge to main.

WON'T DO — not viable on KFP, reverted. OUTCOME: attempted the in-cluster MySQL->PostgreSQL migration (PR #205, deployed). It broke KFP on the live cluster: the cache-server CrashLoopBackOff'd with 'Driver pgx is not supported'. ROOT CAUSE (verified against the local kubeflow/pipelines master checkout, not just 2.16.1): the KFP cache-server has NO PostgreSQL support in ANY version — backend/src/cache/ contains zero postgres/pgx references, imports only gorm.io/driver/mysql, the driver switch handles only case mysql -> Fatalf, and gorm.Open(mysql.Open()) is hardcoded. The api-server (driver 'pgx') and MLMD DO support Postgres, but cache-server is a hard blocker, so an all-Postgres backend is impossible on current KFP. A hybrid (Postgres for api+MLMD, MySQL for cache) was rejected — it doubles DB infra and defeats the consolidation goal. RESOLUTION: reverted to MySQL (PR #208), restored service, and additionally hardened the MySQL root password (PR #209, bd platform-3mr). VERIFIED END-TO-END live on archegos-dev-eks: mysql + cache-server + metadata-grpc + ml-pipeline all 1/1 Running, clients auth with the generated password (no errors), and a test pipeline ran PENDING->RUNNING->SUCCEEDED (exercising mlpipeline/metadb/cachedb). Orphaned postgres resources cleaned up. NOT re-attemptable until upstream adds cache-server Postgres support; re-open/file a new bead if that lands.
